---
title: "Example Technical Note"
description: "An unpublished template used to verify Quarto math, cross-references, and theorem rendering."
date: 2026-07-14
draft: true
---

{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

## Setup

Let $X_1,\ldots,X_n$ be independent random variables satisfying
$\E[X_i]=0$ and $\E[X_i^2]<\infty$. Define

$$
S_n = \sum_{i=1}^n X_i.
$$ {#eq-partial-sum}

::: {#thm-variance-additivity}

## Variance additivity

Under the assumptions above,

$$
\E[S_n^2] = \sum_{i=1}^n \E[X_i^2].
$$ {#eq-variance-additivity}

:::

::: {.proof}

Expanding the square gives

$$
\begin{aligned}
\E[S_n^2]
&= \E\left[\left(\sum_{i=1}^n X_i\right)^2\right] \\
&= \sum_{i=1}^n \E[X_i^2]
 + 2\sum_{1 \le i < j \le n}\E[X_iX_j].
\end{aligned}
$$ {#eq-square-expansion}

For $i\ne j$, independence and centering imply
$\E[X_iX_j]=\E[X_i]\E[X_j]=0$. Substituting into
@eq-square-expansion proves @thm-variance-additivity.

:::

By @eq-variance-additivity and Chebyshev's inequality, for every $t>0$,

$$
\Prob\!\left(\abs{S_n}\ge t\right)
\le
\frac{\sum_{i=1}^n \E[X_i^2]}{t^2}.
$$ {#eq-chebyshev}
